purpose: Promote newly ingested arXiv frontier papers into useful MoTe2-centered graph structure with explicit claims, mechanisms, and open questions.

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; useful for the superconducting neighborhood of the nu=-2/3 FCI.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; establishes all-optical control of spin-valley and Chern order in twisted MoTe2.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; overlaps the optical-control story but adds domain-wall writing and bistate cycling.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; important electric-field phase-transition paper in the MoTe2/WSe2 moire platform.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; extends the phase diagram into a larger-angle reconstructed Chern-insulator regime.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; key twist-angle phase-evolution paper linking FQAH/CFL regimes to valley-degenerate superconductivity.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

metadata.curation_note: Promoted after arXiv abstract review; useful theory link between magnon topology, displacement field, and Tc in moire Chern ferromagnets.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: angle-aligned MoTe2/WSe2 moire heterobilayer
nodes.summary: Moire heterobilayer platform where displacement field tunes Mott, QAH, and antiferromagnetic valley-coherent states at fixed filling.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: optical spin-valley switching
nodes.summary: Resonant circularly polarized optical pumping can reverse the spin-valley orientation of correlated MoTe2 Chern ferromagnets without an applied magnetic field.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: topological magnon spectrum
nodes.summary: The topology and gap of low-energy magnon bands can strongly affect magnetic stability and transition temperature in moire Chern ferromagnets.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: valley-degenerate superconductivity
nodes.summary: Superconductivity emerging at larger twist angle in twisted bilayer MoTe2 after suppression of the small-angle valley-polarized fractional-topology regime.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: reconstructed Chern insulator
nodes.summary: Chern-insulating state in a larger-angle twisted MoTe2 regime where bandwidth is enhanced and the usual small-angle fractional hierarchy is reconstructed.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: composite Fermi liquid
nodes.summary: Compressible correlated metallic state expected near fractional Chern regimes and explicitly reported as a candidate half-filling phase in twisted bilayer MoTe2.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Doping the nu=-2/3 FCI can yield a holon metal, charge-density wave, and chiral superconductor rather than a simple large-FS Fermi liquid.
nodes.summary: The 2025 arXiv theory frames superconductivity adjacent to the nu=-2/3 FQAH state in twisted MoTe2 as emerging from a strongly correlated doped-FCI problem, not just from a conventional large-Fermi-surface metal.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: What is the normal state adjacent to the nu=-2/3 superconducting neighborhood in twisted MoTe2?
aliases: large Fermi surface versus correlated metal
nodes.summary: The theoretical arXiv paper makes explicit that the proximate normal state is still unresolved: it may be a conventional Fermi liquid, a low-carrier-density correlated metal, or something more directly inherited from the doped FCI.

provenance.method: Codex curation inference from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Circularly polarized light can non-thermally reverse spin-valley order and switch Chern order in twisted MoTe2 at zero field.
nodes.summary: The 2025 arXiv report says resonant excitation of the attractive polaron transition dynamically reverses the spin-valley orientation of correlated MoTe2 phases, giving direct optical control of a topological order parameter at zero magnetic field.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Optical switching of integer and fractional Chern ferromagnets enables bistate cycling and domain-wall writing in twisted MoTe2.
nodes.summary: The companion 2025 arXiv paper reports zero-field optical switching of integer and fractional Chern ferromagnets with low pump power, plus magnetic bistate cycling and spatially resolved writing of ferromagnetic domain walls.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: At nu=1, MoTe2/WSe2 shows two displacement-field-driven topological phase transitions between frustrated, QAH-Mott, and valley-coherent antiferromagnetic insulators.
nodes.summary: The 2026 arXiv report says increasing displacement field drives an angle-aligned MoTe2/WSe2 moire heterobilayer from a geometrically frustrated Mott insulator to a ferromagnetic QAH Mott insulator and then to an antiferromagnetic valley-coherent Mott state, with the first transition occurring without an observed charge-gap closure.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Around 4.54 degrees, twisted bilayer MoTe2 hosts reconstructed C=1 insulators and a field-induced nu=-2/3 FCI.
nodes.summary: The 2026 arXiv report extends tMoTe2 into a moderately correlated larger-angle regime, finding C=1 insulators at nu=-1, -0.53, and -1/2, plus a magnetic-field-induced fractional Chern insulator near nu=-2/3 accompanied by an insulator-metal transition.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Increasing twist angle drives twisted bilayer MoTe2 from valley-polarized fractional topology into valley-degenerate superconductivity.
nodes.summary: The 2026 arXiv transport study reports an evolution across 3.8 to 5.78 degrees from Jain-sequence FQAH states and related valley-polarized phases at smaller angle to a larger-angle superconducting regime adjacent to correlated insulators and resembling twisted bilayer WSe2.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Small-angle devices show an anomalous composite Fermi liquid signature near half filling alongside Jain-sequence FQAH states.
nodes.summary: The same 2026 twist-angle paper says that at relatively small twist angle the partially filled Chern band hosts Jain-sequence FQAH states together with an anomalous composite Fermi liquid candidate near moire hole filling nu_h=1/2.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract

nodes.label: Displacement-field-tuned magnon topology can strongly enhance Tc in moire Chern ferromagnets at nu=-1.
nodes.summary: The 2026 arXiv theory argues that the magnon spectrum undergoes a topological transition with displacement field and that the quantum anomalous Hall phase can support an order-of-magnitude larger magnetic transition temperature than a topologically trivial correlated insulator.

provenance.method: Codex curation pass from arXiv abstract
